author: @leerogers6423
text:
I've restored a few Drummond lathes one of which had the original foot powered treadle and fly wheel. 
Having used it I'm quite convinced that if a chief engineer had a choice of apprentices on his section that 
he'd choose the one with legs like a cart horse over one with  any brains.

author: @rolandshelley5165
text: The reason people make single purpose versións is because they aré more efficient.

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank you soooo much,as always youre inspiring!!
Teachers with a sense of humour as well as a passion for the subject,
        are  the teachers we remember as well as learn from!
You Sir are all these things and much more!!
I adore that infectious laugh,but Im not telling the hubby,as Im encouraging him to learn from you! :)X

author: @X02switchblades

author: @AdrianP247
text:
We have a great many people voluntarily going to the gym and jumping on exer-cycles.
We need to put some attachments on, and get all that energy to work!

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text: Im thinking of starting a gymnasium,built on this principle!!

replies.author: @AdrianP247
replies.text: @AndreaDingbatt You could also insert some piezo elements and a capacitor into a runner's shoe. Then those pesky joggers could be charging up their ipod while they are training for their next marathon

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
@AdrianP247 Lol 🤣It took me a moment or two to understand what you were saying!! 😁
I'm giving this some serious thought! 👍
I am really thinking that I would need someone with the technical knowledge, like yourself. 🙂.
Also I think about all those treadmills, and how much folks pay for the Gym memberships, and personal trainers....
I think this could be a viable option, if done well.
Namaste. XxX

author: @CairnOfDunnCroftPermaculture
text: I have something similar in mind, but powered by a simple VAWT. It's very rarely still where I live, wind is pretty constant. I'll have it powering a sort of universal frame I can plug various things into, like a compost sieve or biochar grinder.

author: @mmfc6
text:
I can see something that mounts to the bottom of a kitchen counter that swings underneath to get out of the way. It could power a coffee grinder, blender, food processer etc. 
It appeared that at 7:48, when you stopped pedaling the dynapod stopped instantly. A larger flywheel and a freewheel might even out the powerstrokes.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: for sure mate - a flywheel is a certain improvement

author: @johnnorris1983
text:
Yes but as a human I'm intrinsically lazy!
Can't I get a very tall pendulum as at the British museum that goes on for weeks without a push. Sit this in some argon pond and float polystyrene foam break-offs in it and get just enough energy to light the home with the static produced from a simple push..

replies.author: @johnnorris1983
replies.text: Yes the pond would Need to be shielded from earth ground.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Humans aren't intrinsically lazy mate - to be honest they are at their best when working

replies.author: @johnnorris1983
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 
I don't disagree but with me getting old there's less I can do in a day. I'm only after a cheep easy "free-ish" 12V hardwired light source light source that may see me through my dotage. I'd love to see me peddling the dynamo at 85 ?

replies.author: @johnnorris1983
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkeringp.s. I like to busy myself anyway but don't get time in the day to do it All. Isn't that lazy in some respects? Doesn't it seem to become so?

replies.author: @johnnorris1983
